seoTitle: Add Time with the TIME Function in Google Sheets
metaDescription: Learn how to use the TIME function in Google Sheets to add hours, minutes, and seconds accurately for scheduling, timesheets, and project tracking.
intro: Calculating work hours, scheduling events, or managing time zones by hand is tedious and easy to get wrong. This guide explains how to use the TIME function in Google Sheets to add time correctly, so your calculations stay accurate whether you're tracking billable hours or planning a schedule.

steps.heading: Understand how the TIME function works
steps.body: The TIME function combines hours, minutes, and seconds into a properly formatted time value that Sheets can calculate with, rather than treating it as plain text.

steps.heading: Apply it to practical examples
steps.body: Use the function to calculate work hours, schedule events, or manage time zones for global teams, turning raw numbers into usable time data.

steps.heading: Avoid common mistakes
steps.body: Watch out for mixing text and time formats, or entering values that Sheets misinterprets, which are common sources of errors when adding time.

steps.heading: Integrate the TIME function with other formulas
steps.body: Combine TIME with other Sheets functions to build more advanced reports, such as calculating total hours worked across a project.

steps.heading: Apply this to real-world business scenarios
steps.body: Time calculations are useful across project management, accounting, and any workflow where accurate time tracking matters.

faq.question: What does the TIME function actually calculate?
faq.answer: It builds a time value from hours, minutes, and seconds you specify, which Sheets can then add, subtract, or use in further calculations.

faq.question: Can I use TIME to calculate total work hours?
faq.answer: Yes, this is one of the most common use cases, letting you add up shifts or project time entries accurately.

faq.question: What mistakes should I avoid with the TIME function?
faq.answer: A common mistake is entering time as plain text instead of using the function or proper time formatting, which can cause calculation errors.

faq.question: Is the TIME function useful for managing time zones?
faq.answer: It can support time zone-related calculations when combined with other formulas, which is helpful for teams working across different regions.
